title: The Evolution of Nucleic Acid Discrimination: Data and Code
description: Computational analysis demonstrating that ancient protein domains in LUCA's proteome were generalist nucleic acid binders that did not distinguish between RNA and DNA. Includes census of LUCA's NA-binding proteome (Wehbi x RBPWorld Pfam intersection), NAS-Bench structural specificity analysis, ancestral state reconstruction for 19 domain families, and convergence analysis of ancestral binding modes.
notes: This dataset accompanies the manuscript 'The Evolution of Nucleic Acid Discrimination'. Structural prediction outputs (AF3, 14GB) are available separately upon request.
